key_insight: The paper replaces expensive iterative topology optimization with a flow-matching generative model that learns to synthesize feasible structures, especially by using BESO optimization trajectories as physics-aware training paths.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster candidate design generation for structural topology optimization under changing constraints, with better compliance and volume-fraction control than diffusion-based sampling.
method: Linear FMTO: interpolate from Gaussian noise to a BESO reference topology as an endpoint-based baseline.
Trajectory-aware FMTO: build probability paths from volume-fraction-indexed BESO states to encode optimization history.
Learn a target velocity field from these paths so inference needs no extra optimization or guidance.
evidence_signal: Experiments report improved compliance-related performance, topology fidelity, and volume-fraction satisfaction with far fewer sampling steps than a diffusion baseline, and moderate trajectory weighting performs best under limited data.
watch_item: Too much trajectory guidance can over-constrain transport and reduce generative flexibility, so path weighting and anchor density need careful tuning.
paper_title: Trajectory-Aware Flow Matching for Topology Optimisation
